draft.title: ai workspace
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
ai workspace

Building your first draft…

facade.hypothesis: Users building an AI workspace prefer organic, approachable forms over sharp geometric precision
facade.label: Soft rounded corners
facade.acceptImplies: Explore warmer, friendlier interface elements; soften the computational aesthetic
facade.rejectImplies: Lean into clean minimalism, crisp edges, and functional clarity; embrace the digital/technical character

facade.hypothesis: Users building AI tools prefer dark interfaces for reduced eye strain and focus during long coding sessions
facade.label: Dark workspace
facade.acceptImplies: Explore dark color palettes, high contrast modes, and nighttime-optimized themes for the workspace
facade.rejectImplies: Test bright, light, or daytime-optimized interfaces instead

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer a friendly, chat-like voice over formal corporate language in their AI workspace
facade.label: Conversational tone
facade.acceptImplies: The workspace should feel approachable and human—use contractions, casual phrasing, and warm language in prompts, buttons, and system messages
facade.rejectImplies: The workspace should maintain professional distance—formal language, clear business tone, minimal personality to stay neutral and corporate

evidence.hypothesis: Users building an AI workspace prefer organic, approachable forms over sharp geometric precision
evidence.implication: Explore warmer, friendlier interface elements; soften the computational aesthetic

facade.hypothesis: Users building AI workspaces prefer continuous, eased motion over instant state changes
facade.label: Smooth scroll, no jump
facade.acceptImplies: Workspace should feel like a flowing environment—panning, zooming, and transitions ease in and out; gestures carry weight
facade.rejectImplies: Workspace should snap between states instantly; interaction feels immediate and direct, like clicking; no tweening or easing

facade.hypothesis: Users building AI workspaces prefer metrics and statistics shown upfront over narrative explanations
facade.label: Numbers first
facade.acceptImplies: Dashboards, KPIs, charts, real-time counters, and numeric summaries should dominate information hierarchy
facade.rejectImplies: Narrative descriptions, guided tours, contextual explanations, and prose-based documentation should lead instead

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimal, distraction-free visual design vs. rich, detailed interface aesthetics for their workspace
Whether user wants tools always visible and organized in panels vs. hidden away and revealed on demand
Whether user responds to encouraging, conversational guidance vs. neutral, straightforward instruction in their workspace
Whether user wants a calm, focused atmosphere vs. energetic, stimulating environment for work
Whether user prefers information presented in dense, comprehensive views vs. simplified, progressive disclosure
Whether user wants smooth, animated transitions that feel responsive vs. instant, snappy interactions without flourish

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers minimal, distraction-free visual design vs. rich, detailed interface aesthetics for their workspace
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants tools always visible and organized in panels vs. hidden away and revealed on demand
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user responds to encouraging, conversational guidance vs. neutral, straightforward instruction in their workspace
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants a calm, focused atmosphere vs. energetic, stimulating environment for work
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers information presented in dense, comprehensive views vs. simplified, progressive disclosure
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants smooth, animated transitions that feel responsive vs. instant, snappy interactions without flourish
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: Users building AI workspaces prefer persistent left navigation paired with card-based content areas rather than tab-based layouts
facade.axisTargeted: Navigation persistence and content grouping strategy
facade.label: Sidebar with cards
facade.acceptImplies: Explore variations: collapsible sidebar depths, card density, sidebar width preferences, card spacing
facade.rejectImplies: Test tab-based and list-based alternatives; investigate horizontal navigation patterns

facade.hypothesis: Users building an AI workspace prefer cool, precise digital aesthetics over warm organic forms, valuing transparency and technical clarity in the interface
facade.label: Glass and steel
facade.acceptImplies: Lean into sleek, translucent materials and minimalist geometry; embrace the computational nature of AI tools
facade.rejectImplies: Reinforce warm, tactile, approachable design language; mask technical complexity behind friendly surfaces

draft.title: AI Workspace Hub
draft.summary: A focused workspace interface for managing AI tasks, projects, and collaborative workflows. Clean, minimal design with warm neutrals and intuitive navigation.
